eaip:DocumentMeta.eaip:Title: Enterprise AI Agent Interoperability Protocol (EAIP) — Technical Specification
eaip:DocumentMeta.eaip:Abstract: This specification defines the mandatory transport, identity, state-management, governance, and auditing requirements for secure, stateful, and auditable communication between heterogeneous AI agents across enterprise boundaries. It mandates gRPC transport, SPIFFE/SPIRE identity, RCE state persistence, OPA-based policy enforcement, and Kafka WORM-based audit logging.
eaip:DocumentMeta.eaip:StandardizationNecessity: Standardization is critical for ensuring interoperability between diverse AI agents, maintaining security through zero-trust identities, and enabling auditable, reproducible decision-making pipelines in complex enterprise environments.

eaip:Protocol.eaip:Name: gRPC
eaip:Protocol.eaip:Rationale: gRPC provides binary efficiency, native streaming support, and schema-first contracts necessary for agentic workflows. REST and WebSockets are explicitly prohibited for intra-mesh communication to minimize latency and ensure consistency.

eaip:IdentityLayer.eaip:IAMArchitecture: Leverages SPIRE (SPIFFE Runtime Environment) for automated identity issuance and mTLS-based authentication between autonomous agents.

eaip:PolicyEnforcement.eaip:Description: Every agent request MUST be validated against a Rego-based policy prior to execution. Policies are dynamically updated via a central control plane.

eaip:GovernanceLayer.eaip:ComplianceAsCode: Mandates that all agent behavioral constraints are expressed as version-controlled code and enforced at the protocol level.

eaip:AuditPersistence.eaip:Description: All EAIP-compliant agents MUST emit a trace event for every request/response cycle to an immutable Kafka topic. This ensures a "memory trace map" for forensic and governance purposes.

eaip:EnterpriseAIProtocol.eaip:SequenceDiagram:
sequenceDiagram %% EAIP v2.5.0 — Full request lifecycle with Governance & Auditing participant Client as Client Service participant Gateway as Inference Gateway participant SPIRE as SPIRE Agent participant OPA as OPA Policyd participant Kafka as Kafka WORM Audit
rect rgb(245, 245, 240) Note over Client,SPIRE: §2 Identity — SVID Acquisition Client->>SPIRE: FetchX509SVID() SPIRE-->>Client: X509-SVID Gateway->>SPIRE: FetchX509SVID() SPIRE-->>Gateway: X509-SVID end
rect rgb(240, 245, 245) Note over Client,Gateway: §1 Transport — gRPC/mTLS Channel Client->>Gateway: TLS 1.3 ClientHello (SPIFFE SVID) Gateway-->>Client: TLS 1.3 ServerHello end
rect rgb(245, 240, 245) Note over Client,OPA: §4 Governance — Policy Check Client->>OPA: CheckPolicy(spiffeId=client, action=request) OPA-->>Client: PolicyDecision { allow: true } end
Note over Client,Kafka: §5 Auditing — Initial Trace Client->>Kafka: EmitEvent(RCE_header, status=PENDING)
Note over Client,Gateway: §3 RCE — Request Execution Client->>Gateway: InferenceRequest(rce=RCE_depth0) activate Gateway Gateway->>Kafka: EmitEvent(RCE_header, status=PROCESSING) Gateway-->>Client: InferenceResponse(rce=RCE_depth0_updated) deactivate Gateway
Note over Client,Kafka: §5 Auditing — Final Trace Client->>Kafka: EmitEvent(RCE_header, status=COMPLETED)

eaip:Conformance.eaip:Requirement: All events logged to Kafka WORM.
